source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the diagram, in $\triangle ABC$, $D$ and $E$ are points on $AB$ and $AC$ respectively, and $DE \parallel BC$. If $AE:EC = 1:4$, then what is the value of $\frac{S_{\triangle ADE}}{S_{\triangle BEC}}$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Since $\frac{AE}{EC}=\frac{1}{4}$,\\
thus $\frac{{S}_{\triangle ABE}}{{S}_{\triangle EBC}}=\frac{1}{4}$,\\
thus ${S}_{\triangle ABE}=\frac{1}{4}{S}_{\triangle EBC}$,\\
Since DE$\parallel$BC,\\
thus $\frac{AD}{DB}=\frac{AE}{EC}=\frac{1}{4}$,\\
thus $\frac{{S}_{\triangle ADE}}{{S}_{\triangle BDE}}=\frac{1}{4}$,\\
thus ${S}_{\triangle BDE}=4{S}_{\triangle ADE}$,\\
Also, since ${S}_{\triangle BDE}={S}_{\triangle ABE}-{S}_{\triangle ADE}$,\\
thus $4{S}_{\triangle ADE}=\frac{1}{4}{S}_{\triangle EBC}-{S}_{\triangle ADE}$,\\
thus $\frac{{S}_{\triangle ADE}}{{S}_{\triangle EBC}}=\boxed{\frac{1}{20}}$.

requires_image=true and text_dominant=false in the sample fields

answer_text uses multiple linked deductions: AE:EC -> area ratio, DE parallel BC -> proportional segments, then subtraction/composition of areas
the problem is open-response with no choices, so solving requires constructed reasoning rather than recognition

answer_text provides an explicit derivation path that can be step-checked against geometric rules

normalization_record.normalized_question_text: As shown in the diagram, in $\triangle ABC$, $D$ and $E$ are points on $AB$ and $AC$ respectively, and $DE \parallel BC$. If $AE:EC = 1:4$, then what is the value of $\frac{S_{\triangle ADE}}{S_{\triangle BEC}}$?
normalization_record.normalized_answer_text: Since $\frac{AE}{EC}=\frac{1}{4}$, thus $\frac{S_{\triangle ABE}}{S_{\triangle EBC}}=\frac{1}{4}$, so ${S}_{\triangle ABE}=\frac{1}{4}{S}_{\triangle EBC}$. Since $DE\parallel BC$, $\frac{AD}{DB}=\frac{AE}{EC}=\frac{1}{4}$, thus $\frac{S_{\triangle ADE}}{S_{\triangle BDE}}=\frac{1}{4}$, so ${S}_{\triangle BDE}=4S_{\triangle ADE}$.
